To create a request, use the Storyblok

As API requests must be authenticated, you’ll need to provide an access token. The Content Delivery API requires a read-only access token, whilst the Management API requires either an
access token. You can learn more about authentication in the

The Content Delivery and Management APIs have different rate limits depending on the
, these are expressed in requests per second.
to slow down the API requests by introducing intermediate delays. You can specify the maximum number of requests per second allowed:
for the Content Delivery API and
As the rate limit can differ on the type of request, and you can only configure
instance, you’ll need to create a new session when you need to make requests with a different rate limit.
Be careful when using multiple sessions concurrently as the requests sent to the API from their combined usage may still exceed the rate limit.
If you do exceed the rate limit, the API will respond with HTTP status error code 429, see

content.text: Configuring default parameters for all requests

inlineContent.text: Default parameters are only available for the Content Delivery API.
that are applied to all requests:

code: version: .draft, // the version of resources to retrieve

inlineContent.text: By specifying a default value for the
parameter you can retrieve a specific
of a published resource, otherwise, the
parameter will be automatically set the to the latest version of the space after the first request to the Content Delivery API.
The responses from the Content Delivery API are cached locally by
, both in memory and on disk. To serve subsequent requests for the same resource from the cache instead of performing a network request, the Storyblok
for the Storyblok APIs you can create requests using

Requests to the Storyblok APIs can fail due to network errors or the API responding with an HTTP status error code. These failures include transient errors which can be recovered by retrying the request.
You can use Combine to easily
and with the help of the
operator you can also retry recoverable HTTP status error codes returned by the API:

code: case Api.ResponseError.client(let statusCode, let data, let response): switch(statusCode) {
case 400: fatalError("The wrong format was sent (e.g., XML instead of JSON).")
case 401: fatalError("No valid API key was provided.")
case 404: fatalError("The requested resource doesn’t exist (e.g., due to not yet published content entries).")
case 422: fatalError("The request was unacceptable, often due to missing a required parameter.")
case 429: fatalError("Too many requests hit the API too quickly.")
return Empty<(data: Data, response: URLResponse), Never>()

inlineContent.text: The pattern in the example above can be used as a robust way to handle failed requests:
returns a publisher that will publish a
on recieving a tranisent error response from the API.
will reattempt the request up to five times on any errors published by
on recieving any other error response from the API.
will handle any errors published by the preceeding
, in addition to errors thrown by the preceeding
in the case the fifth and final retry also resulted in failure.
In the catch closure you would need to decide how to handle the error; the example above handles
) which would usually indicate programmer error (with the possible exception of
introduce an exponential delay of up to 60 seconds before resuming requests when one fails due to a tranisent error.
The delay is automatically applied before retries and all other pending requests made with the same

All Storyblok APIs responses, including errors, return JSON which you can convert to objects and values
Requests bodies should also be sent as JSON, and the content type of requests is automatically set to

code: let body = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as! [String: Any]
let body = try JSONDecoder().decode(Body.self, from: data)

An OAuth Access Token is obtained via the OAuth2 authentication flow and is tied to a single space.
A custom API server location you specify.
A Personal Access Token is obtained from the Storyblok UI and grants access to all spaces associated with your account
